description: The fixed plan of installment entries that a Payment must settle, built from the agreed PaymentTerms when the supplier/PO is finalised. It owns the locked-schedule guard: once any installment is APPROVED, PARTIAL_APPROVAL or PROCESSED the schedule is locked and existing entries can no longer be edited or deleted (axiom A9), protecting committed money. One Payment has one PaymentSchedule; the schedule has many Installments.

fields.name: identifier
fields.label: Schedule Ref.
fields.validation: configurable pattern; unique; immutable once created
fields.purpose: Stable key that ties the schedule to its parent Payment and lets each Installment reference one owning schedule; the anchor every schedule edit, lock check, and audit entry resolves against.

fields.name: paymentId
fields.label: Payment
fields.validation: Payment exists; one schedule per payment (1:1); read-only after creation
fields.purpose: Links the schedule to the settlement record it plans so the paid/upcoming/overdue breakdown and the maker/checker release gate operate on one payable. The 1:1 owner of all installment entries.

fields.name: poId
fields.label: Purchase Order
fields.mandatoryCondition: set when the schedule settles against a PO (credit/advance track); null on a cash-float buy which has no PO reference
fields.validation: PO exists; null only permitted for the cash-float track
fields.purpose: Links the schedule to the order whose terms it implements so advance recoupment, retention release, and commitment relief can be traced to that PO. Null is the explicit marker of the no-PO cash branch.

fields.name: terms
fields.label: Payment Terms
fields.validation: one of the locked PaymentTerms list; no free-text terms; agreed at sourcing award (diagram 04)
fields.purpose: The agreed commercial terms (100% advance / part-advance + against documents / part-advance + against shipment / 30/70 / net 30 / net 60 / net 90) that determine how many installments exist, their due events, and their amounts. A locked allowed list, never free text; about 90% of local and US supplies are net 90.

fields.name: currency
fields.label: Currency
fields.validation: a configured currency master; inherited from the parent payment/PO
fields.purpose: The schedule currency every installment amount is denominated in; the base-currency siblings are FX-converted from it so release thresholds and aging are normalized (axiom A12).

fields.name: totalAmount
fields.label: Schedule Total
fields.validation: = sum of agreed installment amounts; recomputed on any editable-schedule change
fields.purpose: The full value the schedule must settle; reconciles to Payment.totalAmount and is the denominator for the paid/upcoming/overdue/pending breakdown. Recomputed only while the schedule is still editable.

fields.name: locked
fields.label: Schedule Locked
fields.validation: true iff any installment.status in {APPROVED, PARTIAL_APPROVAL, PROCESSED}; recomputed on each installment transition; when true, upsert and delete of existing entries are silently skipped (no error) and only null/RESCHEDULED entries are editable; new schedules (no id) are always created
fields.purpose: The guard that protects committed money: once any installment has been approved or processed the schedule is frozen so amounts and entries an approver has acted on cannot be silently changed (axiom A9). The lock is the core integrity rule of the settlement leg.

fields.name: financialRevertOnEdit
fields.label: Reverts Finance Approval on Edit
fields.validation: fixed rule: editing payTerms or paymentSchedules reverts auto-approved Finance/Management completions to AWAITING_APPROVAL
fields.purpose: Encodes the financial-info revert rule (axiom A7): a change to the schedule or terms re-opens any auto-approved Finance/Management approval stage so the new terms are re-reviewed rather than carrying a stale approval.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.label: Created At
fields.purpose: Timestamps when the schedule was fixed against the finalised supplier/PO; the reference point for aging and for ordering the audit trail of schedule changes.

invariants: A9 schedule locked (no delete/edit of existing entries) if any installment in {APPROVED, PARTIAL_APPROVAL, PROCESSED}; one remainder per partial
A7 editing payTerms/paymentSchedules reverts auto-approved Finance/Management stage to AWAITING_APPROVAL
A13 audit + SSE on every committed change
